methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Arondite is a Defense & Security sector company at the Seed stage founded by Will Blyth and Rob Underhill. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS), with focus on 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies: Tenna Systems ($60M Seed), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), and Stark Defence ($500M Series A).

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses Arondite's $12M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $30M valuation. The Peer-Median method triangulates across the 4-company comparable set, which spans $60M to $500M with a median of $225M, directly implied as the peer benchmark. The disparity between these two methods reflects the wide range of outcomes at early stages within this sector.

The triangulated analysis yields a low estimate of $26M, a median of $128M, and a high of $259M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around capital intensity, customer concentration, and regulatory pathway maturity in Defense & Security. Stage-Progression anchors the conservative end; peer multiples—particularly Series A data—anchor the high end. Given Arondite's Seed status relative to Series A comparables, the $26M–$128M band represents the more defensible range absent further operational or market validation.
